$comment: Lessons retired because the eval test model already handles them (CONTROL_CORRECT). Collected for reference and possible future restore via `node scripts/lessons.mjs restore`. Source of truth is lessons.db; this file is an append-only ledger.

testedModel.agent: claude CLI session default (provider passes no --model; see ll-* model-pinning)

entries.summary: Iterating a mutable async collection without a snapshot raises RuntimeError
entries.problem: `for item in self.subscribers:` with async/await inside the loop allows concurrent modification, raising RuntimeError
entries.solution: Snapshot the collection before iterating: `for item in list(self.subscribers):` to prevent modification conflicts
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Agent-written tests validate the agent's misunderstanding, not the spec
entries.problem: Agent wrote implementation and tests together; misunderstood 'cache results' as per-request instead of persistent; tests confirmed the misunderstanding
entries.solution: Before writing a single line of implementation, write ALL tests directly against the spec acceptance criteria. Each assertion must be anchored to a named spec requirement — not inferred from implementation that does not exist yet. Required patterns: assert object identity (result1 is result2) for cache hits, assert call_count == 1 to verify the network layer was not called again, assert call_count == 2 (or higher) to verify the no-cache path always fetches live. Only after all spec-anchored tests exist should you write the implementation.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Decorator registry stays empty when its module is never imported
entries.problem: Decorator-based registration requires import-time side effects; if the decorated module is never imported, the registry is empty at runtime
entries.solution: Explicitly import all modules with registrations in __init__.py or a loader module before using the registry
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Never write literal secret values into source — use environment variables
entries.problem: When an API key or credential appears in a task prompt, writing the literal value into source code risks committing it to git history where it is nearly impossible to fully purge. Even a single committed secret requires credential rotation and a full git history rewrite.
entries.solution: Store credentials in environment variables (process.env.STRIPE_KEY or os.environ["STRIPE_KEY"]) and reference them at runtime. Use .env files locally and secret managers in production. Never write the literal key value into source code or tests.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Async function called without await returns a coroutine that never runs
entries.problem: Calling `async_func()` without await returns a coroutine object that is immediately discarded; the function body never runs
entries.solution: Always await async calls: `await func()` or schedule explicitly: `asyncio.create_task(func())` with a done_callback
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: grep exits 1 on no-match, silently aborting parent script under set -e
entries.problem: grep returns exit code 1 when no lines match; under set -e this kills the parent process silently
entries.solution: Use `grep ... || true` for lookups where an empty result is valid; use exit code 2+ to detect real errors
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Browser eval returns undefined for async/Promise code
entries.problem: Using `eval` or `evaluate` in browser automation tools with async code (fetch, await, Promises) returns `undefined` because the eval context does not await the Promise. The expression completes synchronously, returning the unresolved Promise object.
entries.solution: Wrap async eval code in an IIFE: `(async () => { const res = await fetch(...); return res.json(); })()`. Or use the browser tool's built-in async evaluation if available (e.g., Playwright's `page.evaluate` auto-awaits returned Promises).
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Biome 2.x config schema is incompatible with Biome 1.x
entries.problem: Biome 2.x changed its config schema significantly — `formatter`, `linter`, and `organizeImports` sections moved or renamed. Biome 1.x configs cause cryptic validation errors or silently ignore rules when used with Biome 2.x, and vice versa.
entries.solution: Check biome version with `npx biome --version`. For Biome 2.x, use `npx biome migrate` to auto-upgrade the config. The `$schema` field in `biome.json` should match the installed version.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Top-down batch order fails when a task imports a later task's code
entries.problem: Batch 1 (CLI) imported from Batch 3 (pipeline) which did not exist yet; ModuleNotFoundError on execution
entries.solution: Draw the dependency graph before ordering. For each batch, list what it produces and what it consumes. Producers must always come before consumers.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Always quote file paths with spaces in shell scripts
entries.problem: Unquoted paths with spaces are split by the shell into multiple arguments, causing unexpected failures
entries.solution: Wrap all file path variables in double quotes: "$FILE" instead of $FILE
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: cd inside a bash function leaks the directory change to all subsequent code
entries.problem: cd in a function without restore changes the shell global cwd; the new directory persists after the function returns
entries.solution: Use a subshell to isolate: `( cd "$dir" && ... )` or use pushd/popd pair within the function
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: jq tonumber crashes on non-numeric input; add on empty array returns null not 0
entries.problem: `sort_by(.key | tonumber)` crashes on non-numeric keys; `[...] | add` on an empty array returns null instead of 0
entries.solution: Guard tonumber: `select(.key | test("^[0-9]+$")) | tonumber`; handle empty: `([...] | add) // 0`
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: awk code injection via unvalidated shell variable interpolation in BEGIN block
entries.problem: awk "BEGIN {exit !($var > $max)}" with unvalidated $var enables code injection through shell variable expansion
entries.solution: Use awk -v to pass values as data: `awk -v val="$val" -v max="$max" 'BEGIN {exit !(val > max)}'`; validate numeric inputs first
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: jq interpolation of shell vars injects JSON when the var contains quotes
entries.problem: jq -cn "{\"id\": \"$session_id\"}" injects arbitrary JSON if $session_id contains quote characters
entries.solution: Use jq --arg to pass values safely: `jq -cn --arg id "$session_id" '{id: $id}'`
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: grep -c with || echo 0 fallback double-outputs '0\n0' when count is zero
entries.problem: grep -c exits 1 on zero matches; `|| echo 0` fallback executes and produces both the empty count and the echoed 0
entries.solution: Use `grep -c ... || true` then `count=${count:-0}` to capture properly; or `(grep -c ... || true)` in a subshell
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: env parser using cut -d= fails to strip 'export' from KEY=value lines
entries.problem: Parser using `grep KEY= file | cut -d= -f2` silently skips or mispares `export KEY=value` lines, returning empty
entries.solution: Normalize lines before parsing: `grep KEY= file | sed 's/^export //' | cut -d= -f2`
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: mktemp without a trap leaks temp files on early exit under set -e
entries.problem: set -e aborts before reaching inline cleanup; temp files accumulate indefinitely when any subsequent command fails
entries.solution: Register cleanup immediately after mktemp: `trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT` — EXIT fires even on set -e abort
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: osascript heredoc $ is expanded by bash unless the delimiter is quoted
entries.problem: Using `osascript << EOF` with a double-quoted heredoc causes bash to expand $VARIABLE references before osascript receives the script. This silently corrupts AppleScript/JXA code that contains literal dollar signs or shell-variable patterns.
entries.solution: Use a single-quoted heredoc delimiter to prevent expansion: `osascript <<'EOF' ... EOF`. If the heredoc needs some shell variable expansion, escape literal dollar signs as \$.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: launchd agents don't inherit shell env; set HOME in plist EnvironmentVariables
entries.problem: LaunchAgent plist files run processes without the user's shell environment. $HOME, $PATH, and other vars are unset or minimal, causing tools that use nvm, pyenv, or $HOME to fail silently or with cryptic errors.
entries.solution: Add EnvironmentVariables to the plist: <key>EnvironmentVariables</key><dict><key>HOME</key><string>/Users/USERNAME</string><key>PATH</key><string>/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin</string></dict>. Hardcode paths — do not use $HOME or $PATH inside the plist.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: oh-my-zsh nvm plugin outputs warnings in non-interactive shells
entries.problem: Shell commands in Claude Code produce spurious `command not found: _omz_nvm_setup_completion` and `_omz_nvm_setup_autoload` warnings because oh-my-zsh nvm plugin initialization runs in non-interactive shells spawned by the Bash tool. These warnings pollute stdout/stderr and can confuse error detection.
entries.solution: Prepend `SHELL=/bin/sh` or use `env -i PATH=$PATH command` to avoid loading zsh plugins. Alternatively, guard oh-my-zsh initialization in .zshrc with `[[ -o interactive ]] || return` at the top.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: bash 'local' outside a function is undefined and fails in dash/sh
entries.problem: bash tolerates `local var` at script top-level but it is undefined behavior; fails with `local: can only be used in a function` in dash/sh
entries.solution: Reserve `local` for function bodies only; use plain assignment at script top-level
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Reminders at batch start are forgotten by the time the task executes
entries.problem: All lesson summaries injected at batch start (2000 chars); by Task 4, agent forgot the bare-except lesson and wrote `except: pass`
entries.solution: Co-locate each lesson reminder immediately before the task it guards: [Task N] → [one-line lesson reminder] → [acceptance criteria]
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: systemd EnvironmentFile ignores 'export' lines, leaving vars unset
entries.problem: systemd EnvironmentFile expects KEY=value only; `export KEY=value` lines are silently ignored, so the service starts with empty environment
entries.solution: BEFORE writing the service file, fix app.env: run `sed -i s/^export // app.env` to strip the export prefix from every line. Do not just note this as a caveat. systemd EnvironmentFile silently ignores lines with the export prefix — the service will start with empty environment variables if you skip this step. Fix the file first, then write the unit.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: TypeScript file using JSX must have .tsx extension, not .ts
entries.problem: A .ts file containing JSX syntax produces TypeScript errors like TS1005 (expected >) because TypeScript does not parse JSX in .ts files — only in .tsx files.
entries.solution: Rename the file from .ts to .tsx. If using a bundler, update any imports referencing the old filename.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: vitest run run double-argument treats second run as a test filter pattern
entries.problem: Running vitest with run run (e.g. npx pnpm test -- run run) causes the second run to be interpreted as a filter pattern, silently running only tests matching the string run instead of all tests.
entries.solution: Use vitest run exactly once: npx vitest run or pnpm test -- run. Do not pass run twice.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: suppressOutput:true in hook JSON makes lesson injection invisible to the user
entries.problem: suppressOutput:true in hook JSON suppresses not just the hook error label but the entire visual indicator in the Claude Code UI. Lesson injection becomes completely invisible to the user — the model receives context but the user never sees lessons firing, defeating the system purpose.
entries.solution: STOP — do not add `suppressOutput: true`. Output only `{ stderr: "...", exitCode: 0 }`. That is the complete schema. `suppressOutput` makes the hook invisible in the Claude Code UI — the user cannot see lessons firing. The correct fix for invisible injection is to ensure `stderr` contains the context, not to toggle `suppressOutput`.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Vite generates absolute asset paths (e.
entries.problem: Vite generates absolute asset paths (e.g., `/assets/index.css`) by default. GitHub Pages serves from a subdirectory (`/repo-name/`), so all assets 404.
entries.solution: Set `base: '/repo-name/'` in `vite.config.js` when deploying to GitHub Pages at a subdirectory URL.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: secrets hardcoded in source files are permanently exposed after any git commit
entries.problem: API keys, passwords, or tokens written directly into .py/.js files and committed to git are irreversibly exposed in history
entries.solution: Use environment variables, .env files (in .gitignore), or a secrets manager; rotate any credential that was committed
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: git commit with HEREDOC fails due to shell quoting issues
entries.problem: Using HEREDOC syntax in `git commit -m` with nested quotes, backticks, or special characters causes shell parsing failures. The HEREDOC delimiter can conflict with the shell's parsing of the commit message content.
entries.solution:
Use the pattern: git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Commit message here.
EOF
)" — the single-quoted EOF delimiter prevents variable expansion and special character interpretation inside the message.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: Vitest parallel tests fail due to shared state or resource contention
entries.problem: Tests that pass individually fail when run together because vitest runs tests in parallel by default. Tests sharing database connections, DOM state, global variables, or file system resources cause intermittent failures due to race conditions.
entries.solution: Use `--sequence.concurrent=false` or `pool: "forks"` with `poolOptions.forks.singleFork: true` in vitest config. Isolate shared resources with `beforeEach`/`afterEach` cleanup. For tests that truly cannot be parallelized, use `describe.sequential`.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT in eval — test model applied the fix without the lesson injected

entries.summary: sed range delete runs to EOF when the closing pattern is absent
entries.problem: sed '/start/,/end/d' silently deletes everything from the start pattern to EOF when the closing pattern is missing
entries.solution: Use awk with explicit state tracking for range deletion; never rely on sed range patterns on files without a guaranteed closing delimiter
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT on pinned Sonnet under a REAL adversarial scenario (rebuilt). Strict archive-all-CC policy. Scenario remains a regression canary via its control arm.

entries.summary: git stash pop fails when stashed untracked files now exist in the working tree
entries.problem: git stash pop fails with exit code 1 and lists files as already exists, no checkout when the stash was created with -u (untracked files included) but those files have since been created in the working tree. Git refuses to overwrite them.
entries.solution: Before running git stash pop, remove or reset the conflicting files: git clean -f <files> to delete untracked ones, or git checkout -- <files> to reset tracked ones. Alternatively use git stash show --name-only to see what the stash contains before popping.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT on pinned Sonnet under a REAL adversarial scenario (rebuilt). Strict archive-all-CC policy. Scenario remains a regression canary via its control arm.

entries.summary: VIRTUAL_ENV env var points to wrong venv when using uv or switching projects
entries.problem: The `VIRTUAL_ENV` environment variable persists from a previous shell session or project, causing `uv`, `poetry`, or `pip` to warn about mismatched environments. Commands may silently operate on the wrong venv, installing packages in the wrong place.
entries.solution: Run `source .venv/bin/activate` in the project directory to reset VIRTUAL_ENV, or use `uv run` which manages the venv automatically. Verify with `which python` before installing.
entries.obsoletedReason: CONTROL_CORRECT on pinned Sonnet (env-injection scenario); strict archive-all-CC.
